text: In a forest where red woods toured and wild flowers danced freely, a circle of children from every shade of skin gathered beneath the sun. They named birds in many languages, braided stories from their ancestors into vines, and painted stones with hooks for the earth. Nature didn't ask where they came from - only they listened, respected, and belonged. In that shared silence between rustling leaves, they found a truth deeper than root: We all have a place. And belonged.
